notes: SAR pegged to USD at 3.75 since 1986; no effective FX transmission channel from oil shocks
IQD managed vs USD; oil revenues stabilise but governance risk creates residual FX pressure on import prices
KWD pegged to undisclosed basket (mainly USD); RSAL buffer fully shields FX from oil price shocks
AED pegged to USD at 3.6725 since 1997; no effective FX transmission channel
QAR pegged to USD at 3.64 since 2001; QIA buffer reinforces peg credibility
OMR pegged to USD at 0.3845 since 1986; peg maintained throughout 2014-2016 oil crash
BHD pegged to USD at 0.376 since 1980; GCC monetary support reinforces peg credibility
DZD managed vs USD/EUR basket; oil-fiscal link creates FX depreciation pressure when prices fall; FX controls limit adjustment speed
LYD parallel market premium reflects conflict-driven FX distortions; dual exchange rate system; high uncertainty
Multiple official/unofficial exchange rates under sanctions; extreme FX volatility partly driven by oil export restrictions; import price pass-through very high
EGP experienced large devaluations (2016 approx. 50%; 2022-2023 approx. 50%+); strong oil-FX correlation through Suez Canal revenue and remittance channel
JOD pegged to USD since 1995; peg credibility maintained but reserve adequacy is sensitive to oil-driven regional dynamics and Gulf remittance flows
LBP lost over 95% of value 2019-2023; pre-existing banking crisis amplified by oil import cost pressures; extreme FX volatility
MAD in managed float band since 2018 reform; phosphate and remittance buffers reduce oil-FX correlation; relatively moderate FX transmission
